## Highlights

This release includes two new features: artwork caching and feed migrations.

### Feed Migrations

Podcast hosts move. Previously the only way to repoint a feed was to delete and re-add it, which generated a new slug (breaking the proxy URL your apps were subscribed to) and discarded every cached episode. v0.2.0 adds a two-step **preview → commit** migration flow:

- The proxy URL stays frozen across migration — apps keep working without re-subscription.
- Cached episodes survive when the new host preserves GUIDs (episodes are keyed by `SHA256(GUID)`).
- Preview surfaces a GUID-overlap diff plus soft warnings (zero/low overlap, empty feed) before you commit. Soft warnings can be force-bypassed; hard errors (unreachable, parse failure, in-flight downloads) cannot.

Available via the web UI and `POST /api/feeds/:id/migrate/preview` + `POST /api/feeds/:id/migrate`.

### Artwork caching

Channel artwork is now downloaded and stored on disk, so feeds keep their cover art even after the upstream image host returns 404. A new `GET /artwork/:id` endpoint serves the cached file, and the proxied RSS is rewritten to point at it. The web UI now shows thumbnails in the feed list and on the episodes page, with a **Refresh Artwork** button to force a fresh download when upstream changes.

## IMPORTANT: Reverse Proxy Config Changes

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **nginx reverse proxy users:** artwork caching introduces a new `GET /artwork/:id` route that podcast apps load when fetching the proxied RSS. If your nginx config only proxies `/feeds/` and `/episodes/`, channel artwork will 404 until you also proxy `/artwork/`. Add the following location block (alongside your existing ones):
>
> ```nginx
> location /artwork/ {
>     limit_except GET HEAD {
>         deny all;
>     }
>     proxy_pass http://<your-server-ip>:<port>;
>
>     proxy_set_header Host $host;
>     proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
> }
> ```

If you are running podproxy behind a different reverse proxy (Caddy, Traefik, etc.), make sure the `/artwork/` path is routed to podproxy the same way `/feeds/` and `/episodes/` are.

The Docker image, config format, and database schema are unchanged — existing volumes upgrade in place.
